summary: Iteration 2 verifies the new BYOK (Bring-Your-Own-Key) + output_format features and the backend/frontend refactor (backend/src/* + frontend/src restructure). Backend: 14/14 pytest tests in test_byok_and_formats.py PASS in ~19s — root '/api/' returns {name: 'Forge API', status: 'ok'}, JWT login returns token + user, GET /api/auth/me works with Bearer, POST /api/api-keys creates encrypted key (api_key_encrypted NEVER returned, only masked via bullet chars), GET /api/api-keys returns masked-only listing, DELETE works, invalid mode returns 400, openai-compat without base_url returns 400, all 4 output_formats stream proper content (yaml has 'jobs:'/'on:', scripts has '=== FILE:' + '#!/usr/bin/env bash'/'Makefile', terraform has 'resource "aws_', cloudformation has 'AWSTemplateFormatVersion' or 'Type: AWS::'), BYOK fake Groq key produces meta event with mode='openai-compat' and base_url=https://api.groq.com/openai/v1 followed by upstream 401 auth error (expected — proves BYOK path is wired), non-existent api_key_id returns 404. Frontend: Settings → ApiKeyManager visible, all 5 presets present (Groq, OpenRouter, Together, OpenAI, Anthropic), Groq preset auto-fills base_url + default_model + mode, key creation submits and appears in list with mask + base_url + model, delete works. Pipeline Generator → all 4 output-format buttons present (yaml/scripts/terraform/cloudformation), AI engine dropdown lists Emergent + saved BYOK keys, selecting BYOK swaps model dropdown for custom model input (with key default_model as placeholder), selecting Emergent restores 6-model dropdown, generating with scripts format streams content via SSE.

integration_issues.flow: PipelineGenerator BYOK dropdown after just-in-time key creation
integration_issues.issue: If a user creates an API key in Settings and immediately navigates to /app/pipelines/new in the same page session, the AI-engine dropdown sometimes renders before the /api/api-keys fetch resolves so the newly created key is missing. A page reload makes it appear. Consider refetching userKeys on focus/mount or via a shared context. Not a blocker — the data is fetched correctly; this is a UX race.

action_items: (optional, low priority) Refetch /api-keys in PipelineGenerator on window focus or expose userKeys via context so freshly-created keys appear without a full page reload.

(carry-over from iter1) Refactor done — backend split into src/{controllers,services,...} ✓. Original critical_code_review items about JWT logout/revocation, rate-limiting on /auth/login, and asyncio-based pipeline runner persistence are still open.
critical_code_review_comments: BYOK encryption: /app/backend/src/utils/crypto.py uses Fernet keyed from JWT_SECRET via PBKDF2. api_keys collection projection excludes api_key_encrypted on every read path (controller list + _public helper). Good.
_resolve_stream in pipeline_controller.py decrypts the key on demand and never logs it — good. Mask uses bullet '•' (not '*'); update any client-side regex if it checks for '*'.

stream_anthropic uses AsyncOpenAI against https://api.anthropic.com/v1/ — Anthropic's OpenAI-compat endpoint requires the path /v1/messages-style payloads; chat.completions on Anthropic's compat surface is limited. Real Anthropic BYOK may fail with 404/format errors. Recommend either documenting that 'anthropic' mode expects api.anthropic.com OpenAI-compat OR switching to the anthropic SDK for that branch. Not exercised in tests beyond the path-wiring smoke test.
Stream errors from upstream providers are surfaced as a generic 'error' SSE event with the exception string — fine for MVP, but could include status code separately for UI to display nicely.

PipelineGenIn.custom_model is optional — when BYOK key has no default_model and user submits empty custom_model, /api/pipelines/generate returns 400 'model is required for BYOK'. Frontend should validate this before submit (currently relies on backend 400 + toast).
ApiKeyManager calls window.confirm() for delete — non-Playwright-friendly without dialog handler; tests must register page.on('dialog'). Consider replacing with shadcn AlertDialog for accessibility.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on retest (1 race-condition that resolves with reload)

seed_data_creation: Created 3 BYOK api keys during testing (TEST_UI_groq, TEST_groq_llama3, TEST_to_delete) — all cleaned up. One pre-existing 'Groq LLaMA-3' key created in earlier manual testing remains for the user.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend refactor (backend/src/*) is functional — server.py is a thin re-export. All BYOK + 4 output_format SSE paths work. Mask uses bullet '•' not '*'. Anthropic BYOK branch uses OpenAI-compat against api.anthropic.com/v1/ which may not work with real Anthropic keys for chat.completions — only the openai-compat (Groq) path is verified end-to-end against a real upstream (401 from Groq is the SUCCESS criterion). If extending coverage, test the openai mode against api.openai.com with a real key. Settings page race-condition (newly created BYOK key not appearing immediately in pipeline generator dropdown) is by design — page reload fixes it.
rca of the issue: No backend issues. Minor frontend UX race condition documented above. Initial test failure on BYOK key appearing in PipelineGenerator dropdown was caused by navigating to /app/pipelines/new in the same SPA session before the just-created key's response had fully propagated to the userKeys state; a fresh page load and the direct /api/api-keys fetch both return all keys correctly (verified with status 200, len=3).
